info.title: Cortex SDK API
info.description: REST APIs for Cortex AI retrieval engine

post.description:
Unified endpoint to upload knowledge: documents (files) and/or app-generated sources.

- **Documents**: send one or more files in `files` and optionally `file_metadata`.
- **App sources**: send structured source(s) as JSON in `app_knowledge` (single object or array).

Provide at least one of `files` or `app_knowledge`. You can send both in the same request.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

post.description:
Check the processing status of uploaded content.

Accepts a single ``file_id`` **or** a list of ``file_ids`` (or both).
Returns a ``BatchProcessingStatus`` with one entry per requested ID.

parameters.name: file_id

schema.description: Single file ID (deprecated - use file_ids instead).

schema.description: One or more file IDs to check processing status for.

parameters.description: One or more file IDs to check processing status for.

schema.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

post.description:
Create a tenant for your account (API key auth).

Accepts the request and starts tenant creation in the background, then returns
200 OK immediately. Use GET /tenants/infra/status?tenant_id=... to check
deployment progress.

**Tenant Metadata Schema**

You can optionally provide a `tenant_metadata_schema` to define custom fields that will be
indexed in the vector store. Each field can be configured with:

- `enable_match`: Enable text filtering on this field
- `enable_dense_embedding`: Create dense embeddings for semantic similarity search
- `enable_sparse_embedding`: Create sparse embeddings (BM25) for keyword search

**Example Request:**
```json
{
    "tenant_id": "my-tenant",
    "tenant_metadata_schema": [
        {
            "name": "category",
            "data_type": "VARCHAR",
            "max_length": 256,
            "enable_match": true
        },
        {
            "name": "product_description",
            "data_type": "VARCHAR",
            "max_length": 4096,
            "enable_dense_embedding": true,
            "enable_sparse_embedding": true
        }
    ]
}
```

Expected outcome:
- Request is accepted and tenant creation runs in the background.
- Response returns 200 with status "accepted" and tenant_id.
- Poll GET /tenants/infra/status?tenant_id=... to verify when provisioning is complete.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

get.description:
List unique sub-tenant IDs that have indexed data in Milvus for the given tenant.

Uses API key auth. Returns sub_tenant_id values that appear in the tenant's
context and memory collections (any sub-tenant with at least one chunk).

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

get.description:
List all tenant IDs registered for the authenticated organization (API key auth).

Queries the DynamoDB tenant mapping table for the org and returns the tenant IDs.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

get.description:
Check the status of deployed infrastructure for a tenant.

Returns scheduler, graph (FalkorDB), and vectorstore (Milvus) deployment
status for the given tenant: whether the graph DB is healthy and whether
the context and memory collections exist.

Use this endpoint to verify that tenant provisioning or ArgoCD sync has
completed successfully.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

delete.description:
Delete a tenant and all its associated infrastructure and data.

This permanently removes:
- GitHub ArgoCD infra file (FalkorDB helm values)
- Milvus vector store collections (context + memory)
- MongoDB source documents
- MongoDB chunk_data documents
- MongoDB tenant metadata schema
- DynamoDB primary tenant ID mapping

This action is irreversible.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

get.description:
Retrieve usage stats for your tenant.

Use this endpoint to check whether a tenant exists and view core metrics like total
indexed objects and vector dimension. This helps you validate
setup and monitor ingestion.

Expected outcome

You receive the current object count and vector dimension for the tenant.
If the tenant does not exist, you get a not-found error.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

post.description:
Create an API key (dashboard / NextAuth). Store hashed secret in DynamoDB, return full key once.

Requires NextAuth. The user's email is always taken from the JWT token.
An optional label can be provided for human-readable identification.
Plan is taken from the user's tier for routing.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

post.description:
Upload pre-computed embeddings for advanced similarity search.

This endpoint accepts vector embeddings that you've generated externally,
 allowing you to integrate with custom embedding models or existing vector databases.
  The embeddings represent chunks of your content as numerical vectors.

The system stores these embeddings and makes them available for semantic search and similarity matching.
 Use this when you want to leverage specialized embedding models or have existing vector representations.
When upsert=True, existing embeddings with the same chunk_id will be updated.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

post.description:
Find similar chunks using an embedding vector.

Use this to retrieve the most similar chunk IDs to a single query embedding.

Expected outcome:
- You receive the closest chunk IDs with optional similarity scores.

post.description:
Unified API to list either knowledge sources or user memories.

Supports **pagination** via `page` / `page_size` and optional **filters**
on `tenant_metadata` and `document_metadata`.

- If `source_ids` is provided (non-empty), only those items are returned.
- Otherwise, all items for the tenant/sub-tenant scope are returned.
- Use `type` to switch between listing sources (`knowledge`) vs
  user memories (`memory`).

schema.title: Response List Content List Data Post

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

get.description:
Retrieve relations for a specific source.

Use this endpoint to fetch all relations associated with a specific source.
This is useful when you need to understand the relationships between entities within a source.

Provide the source ID in the request body along with your tenant information to get the relations
for that source.

parameters.name: source_id

schema.description: Source ID. If omitted, returns relations across the entire sub-tenant.

post.description:
Remove documents and content from your knowledge base.

This endpoint permanently deletes the specified sources from your knowledge base.
 Once deleted, the content will no longer be available for search or retrieval.

Use this carefully as the action cannot be undone. The system will confirm
successful deletion of each source ID you specify.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

post.description:
Fetch the content of a source ingested.

This endpoint can return:
- File content directly (as string or base64)
- A presigned URL to access the file
- Both content and presigned URL

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

post.description:
Search for relevant content within your indexed sources (documents).

    Results are ranked by relevance and can be customized with parameters like
    result limits, alpha weighting, and recency preferences.

    Use `mode` to control retrieval quality:
    - "fast" (default): Single query, faster response
    - "thinking": Multi-query generation with reranking, higher quality

    Use `filters` to narrow results by metadata:
    - Provide key-value pairs matching fields defined in your tenant_metadata_schema
    - Example: `{"category": "engineering", "priority": "high"}`
    - Filters are validated against your tenant schema for type safety

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Search for relevant content within user memories (inferred content).

    Results are ranked by relevance and can be customized with parameters like
    result limits, alpha weighting, and recency preferences.

    Use `mode` to control retrieval quality:
    - "fast" (default): Single query, faster response
    - "thinking": Multi-query generation with reranking, higher quality

    Use `filters` to narrow results by metadata:
    - Provide key-value pairs matching fields defined in your tenant_metadata_schema
    - Example: `{"category": "engineering", "priority": "high"}`
    - Filters are validated against your tenant schema for type safety

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Perform full text search for exact matches within your indexed sources or memories.
    Choose between 'OR', 'AND', or 'PHRASE' operators to control how search terms
    are combined for precise text matching.

    Operators:
    - "or"  (default): Match chunks containing **any** of the search terms
    - "and": Match chunks containing **all** of the search terms
    - "phrase": Match chunks containing the **exact phrase** in order

    Use `search_mode` to specify what to search:
    - "knowledge" (default): Search over indexed documents
    - "memory": Search over user memories

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Ask a question and get an AI-generated answer based on your indexed sources or memories.

    The response includes both the AI answer and the source chunks used to generate it,
    enabling full transparency and citation capabilities.

    Use `search_mode` to specify what to search:
    - "knowledge" (default): Search over indexed documents
    - "memory": Search over user memories

    Use `mode` to control retrieval quality:
    - "fast" (default): Single query, faster response
    - "thinking": Multi-query generation with reranking, higher quality

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

429.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

post.description:
Add memories for indexing and retrieval.

    This API provides a unified entry point for ingesting various types of content:

    **Content Types Supported:**
    - Raw text content (`text` field)
    - Markdown formatted content (`text` + `is_markdown=true`)
    - User/assistant conversation pairs (`user_assistant_pairs` field)

    **Key Features:**
    - **Inference Mode (`infer=true`)**: When enabled, the system extracts additional
      insights and implicit information from your content. Useful for conversation
      analysis and knowledge extraction.
    - **Direct Mode (`infer=false`)**: Content is chunked and indexed as-is without
      additional processing.

    Use `/ingestion/verify-processing` endpoint to check the processing status.

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

delete.description:
Permanently remove a specific memory from storage.

    This API allows you to delete a memory by its unique identifier.
    Once deleted, the memory cannot be recovered, so use this operation carefully.

    The memory will be removed from your tenant's storage and will no longer
    appear in search results or memory listings.

parameters.name: memory_id

schema.description: Unique identifier of the memory to delete
schema.title: Memory Id

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the memory to delete

post.description:
Register (or replace) a webhook for this org.

Supply signing_secret (min 16 chars) to have every delivery signed with
X-HydraDB-Signature: sha256=<hmac-sha256>.  Omit to disable signing.
The secret is stored server-side and never returned in API responses.

delete.summary: Delete the registered webhook for this API key
delete.description: Unregister the webhook for this org.

get.description:
Return recent webhook delivery attempts for this API key, newest first.

Use ``next_cursor`` from the response as ``cursor`` on the next call to page through results.

parameters.name: cursor

schema.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.
schema.title: Cursor

parameters.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter by delivery status: pending, failed, delivered, permanently_failed.
schema.title: Status

parameters.description: Filter by delivery status: pending, failed, delivered, permanently_failed.

get.summary: Get a single webhook delivery attempt
get.description: Return the full detail of a single delivery attempt.

post.summary: Retry a failed or permanently failed delivery
post.description:
Reset a failed or permanently_failed delivery so the sweep picks it up again.

Has no effect on deliveries that are already pending, sweeping, or delivered.

Send a synthetic payload to the registered webhook URL right now.

No delivery row is created and no retries are performed.
The payload is signed with your signing_secret if one is configured.
Use this to verify your endpoint is reachable and can verify signatures
before ingesting real documents.

The test payload includes ``"test": true`` so your receiver can distinguish
it from real events.

get.summary: Detect super nodes in the knowledge graph
get.description: Returns entities whose RELATES degree exceeds the given threshold. Useful for diagnosing RAM bloat caused by high-degree nodes.

post.description:
Unified ingestion endpoint for both knowledge and memory.

    Use `type` to specify what you're ingesting:
    - "knowledge" (default): Upload files or app-generated sources for indexing
    - "memory": Add memories (text, conversations) for indexing and retrieval

    **For knowledge (type="knowledge"):**
    Provide `documents` (multipart uploads) and/or `app_knowledge` (JSON sources).

    **For memory (type="memory"):**
    Provide `memories` as a JSON array of memory items in the `memories` field.

Retrieve knowledge graph relations for a specific source or across an entire sub-tenant.

    If `id` is provided, returns relations for that specific document.
    If omitted, returns all relations in the sub-tenant scope.

    Supports cursor-based pagination for large result sets.

post.description:
Unified query endpoint for all retrieval over documents, memories, or both.

    Use `source` to choose what to query:
    - "knowledge" (default): Query over indexed knowledge documents
    - "memory": Query over user memories
    - "all": Query both documents and memories, merge and rank results together

    Use `query_by` to choose the query method:
    - "hybrid" (default): Dense vector + BM25 sparse — best overall relevance
    - "text": BM25 full-text search only — for exact keyword matching

    Use `mode` to control retrieval quality (hybrid only):
    - "fast" (default): Single query, faster response
    - "thinking": Multi-query generation with reranking, higher quality

    Use `operator` to control text matching (text only):
    - "or" (default): Match any of the search terms
    - "and": Match all search terms
    - "phrase": Match the exact phrase

404.description: Not Found - Resource does not exist

results.title: Results

success_count.description: Number of items successfully queued for ingestion.

failed_count.title: Failed Count
failed_count.description: Number of items that failed to queue.

label.description: Human-readable label for the API key (e.g. 'Production - Web App').

scopes.description: Optional list of scopes (e.g. ['ingest', 'query']). Omit or leave empty for now.

env.description: Environment tag embedded into the key (live or test).

api_key_id.title: Api Key Id
api_key_id.description: Identifier used for API key lookup.

env.title: Env
env.description: Environment tag embedded into the key.

full_api_key.title: Full Api Key
full_api_key.description: The full API key (shown only once). Store this securely.

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Origin Cortex source ID. May be filled server-side.

predicate.title: Predicate
predicate.description: Relation type / predicate (e.g. reply_to, parent_of, linked_to).

AppRelationInput.title: AppRelationInput
AppRelationInput.description:
A single explicit relation between two app sources.

* ``source_id``  — the origin Cortex source ID (filled by the route when known).
* ``predicate``  — relation type, e.g. ``"reply_to"``, ``"linked_to"``.
* ``target``     — where the relation points (Cortex ID or external ID).
* ``properties`` — optional bag of relation-level metadata.

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Cortex source ID of the target.

external_id.title: External Id
external_id.description: Provider-scoped external ID when the Cortex source ID is not yet known.

provider.title: Provider
provider.description: Provider namespace for external_id targets (required when external_id is set).

AppRelationTargetRef.title: AppRelationTargetRef
AppRelationTargetRef.description:
Lightweight reference to the target end of a relation.

Either ``source_id`` (an existing Cortex source) or ``external_id`` +
``provider`` (a provider-scoped ID resolved later by ingestion) should be
supplied.

url.title: Url
url.description: Public or internal URL referencing the attachment resource.

title.title: Title
title.description: Human-readable title or filename of the attachment.

content_type.title: Content Type
content_type.description: MIME type of the attachment (e.g., application/pdf).

content_url.title: Content Url
content_url.description: Direct URL for content retrieval when different from the reference URL.

misc.title: Misc
misc.description: Additional attachment attributes defined by the tenant (free-form).

content.description: Structured content payload for the attachment when available.

statuses.title: Statuses
statuses.description: List of processing statuses, one per requested source ID.

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: List of memory items to ingest

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

upsert.title: Upsert
upsert.description: If true, update existing sources with the same source_id.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Optional source ID to filter by (mutually exclusive with chunk_ids)

chunk_ids.description: Optional list of chunk IDs to filter by (mutually exclusive with source_id)

output_fields.description: Optional list of fields to return in results (default: chunk_id, source_id, metadata)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

upsert.title: Upsert
upsert.description: If true, update existing sources with the same id.

documents.title: Documents
documents.description: Documents to upload (knowledge only). Omit when ingesting memories.

document_metadata.title: Document Metadata
document_metadata.description: JSON array of per-file metadata objects (knowledge only). Each object may include: id (optional), metadata, additional_metadata, infer, and relations.

app_knowledge.title: App Knowledge
app_knowledge.description: JSON: single source object or array of app-generated sources (knowledge only).

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: JSON array of memory items (memory only). Each item has text, title, infer, metadata.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

embeddings.title: Embeddings
embeddings.description: List of raw embedding documents to insert

upsert.title: Upsert
upsert.description: If True, update existing embeddings; if False, insert only

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

query_embedding.title: Query Embedding
query_embedding.description: Query embedding vector to search for

limit.title: Limit
limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

filter_expr.title: Filter Expr
filter_expr.description: Optional Milvus filter expression for additional filtering

output_fields.title: Output Fields
output_fields.description: Optional list of fields to return in results (default: chunk_id, source_id, metadata)

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

upsert.title: Upsert
upsert.description: If true, update existing sources with the same id.

files.title: Files
files.description: Files to upload (documents). Omit or leave empty when only sending app_knowledge.

file_metadata.title: File Metadata
file_metadata.description: JSON array of file metadata objects; length must match files when provided. Each object may include: file_id (optional), metadata, additional_metadata, and relations (forceful relations to other cortex source IDs).

app_knowledge.title: App Knowledge
app_knowledge.description: JSON: single source object or array of app-generated sources to index. Omit when only uploading files.

app_sources.title: App Sources
app_sources.description: Deprecated alias for app_knowledge. Use app_knowledge instead.

row_count.title: Row Count
row_count.description: Number of rows in the collection

dimensions.title: Dimensions
dimensions.description: Number of dimensions in the collection

tenant_metadata.title: Tenant Metadata
tenant_metadata.description: Key-value pairs matched against source.tenant_metadata. All provided pairs must match (AND logic).

additional_metadata.title: Additional Metadata
additional_metadata.description: Key-value pairs matched against source.document_metadata (the data you sent as 'additional_metadata' at ingestion). All provided pairs must match (AND logic). Accepts either 'additional_metadata' (canonical) or 'document_metadata' (legacy).

source_fields.title: Source Fields
source_fields.description: Key-value pairs matched against top-level source fields that you supply during ingestion. Allowed keys: description, timestamp, title, type, url. All provided pairs must match (AND logic).

ContentFilter.title: ContentFilter
ContentFilter.description:
Optional filters for narrowing list results.

All filter categories use **AND** logic — every provided key-value pair
must match for a document to be included.

Both ``additional_metadata`` (canonical, matches the ingestion field name)
and ``document_metadata`` (legacy alias) are accepted for the document-
level filter.

Examples
--------
```json
{
    "tenant_metadata": {"department": "Finance", "region": "US"},
    "additional_metadata": {"author": "Alice Smith"},
    "source_fields": {"type": "slack", "title": "standup notes"}
}
```

text.title: Text
text.description: Plain text content extracted or provided for indexing.

html_base64.title: Html Base64
html_base64.description: Base64-encoded HTML content preserving structure and formatting.

csv_base64.title: Csv Base64
csv_base64.description: Base64-encoded CSV data for tabular content ingestion.

markdown.title: Markdown
markdown.description: Raw Markdown content to be indexed as rich text.

files.title: Files
files.description: List of file descriptors associated with the source (e.g., filenames, sizes).

layout.title: Layout
layout.description: Optional layout metadata such as sections or blocks to guide chunking.

data_type.description: Field data type. Accepts friendly names: 'string', 'integer', 'float', 'boolean', 'array', 'object'. Raw internal types are also accepted.

max_length.title: Max Length
max_length.description: Max length for string fields. Increase for longer text content.

enable_analyzer.title: Enable Analyzer
enable_analyzer.description: Enable text analyzer for full-text search capabilities

enable_match.title: Enable Match
enable_match.description: Enable exact keyword filtering on this field

enable_dense_embedding.title: Enable Dense Embedding
enable_dense_embedding.description: Index this field for semantic similarity search. Only applicable to string fields.

enable_sparse_embedding.title: Enable Sparse Embedding
enable_sparse_embedding.description: Index this field for keyword (BM25) search. Only applicable to string fields.

CustomPropertyDefinition.title: CustomPropertyDefinition
CustomPropertyDefinition.description:
Definition for custom/dynamic properties on a collection.

Use this to define tenant metadata fields that can enhance search capabilities:
- enable_dense_embedding: Creates a dense vector field for semantic similarity search
- enable_sparse_embedding: Creates a sparse vector field for BM25 keyword search

Example:
    CustomPropertyDefinition(
        name="product_description",
        data_type="string",
        enable_dense_embedding=True,
        enable_sparse_embedding=True,
    )

success.title: Success
success.description: Indicates whether the memory deletion operation was successful

user_memory_deleted.title: User Memory Deleted
user_memory_deleted.description: Confirms whether the specific memory was successfully deleted

DeleteUserMemoryResponse.title: DeleteUserMemoryResponse
DeleteUserMemoryResponse.description: Response model for deleting a user memory.

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass as `cursor` on the next request to get the next page.

type.title: Type
type.description: PERSON, ORGANIZATION, PROJECT, PRODUCT, ERROR_CODE, etc.

entity_id.title: Entity Id
entity_id.description: Internal unique entity ID from graph database

identifier.title: Identifier
identifier.description: Unique ID like email, employee_id, URL

source_ids.title: Source Ids
source_ids.description: Source IDs to forcefully relate to the uploaded source. Accepts the deprecated alias `cortex_source_ids` for backward compatibility.

properties.title: Properties
properties.description: Optional properties to attach to the forceful relation.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

query.title: Query
query.description: Search terms to find in your content

operator.description: How to combine search terms: 'or' (any term matches), 'and' (all terms must match), 'phrase' (exact phrase must appear)

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

search_mode.description: What to search: 'knowledge' for documents or 'memory' for user memories

synthesis_context.title: Synthesis Context
synthesis_context.description: LLM-generated summary of how sub-query results connect, present only for multi-step queries with requires_synthesis=True.

GraphContext.title: GraphContext
GraphContext.description: Graph context containing query-based paths and chunk-based relation paths.

Infra.title: Infra
Infra.description:
V1 infra shape — ``vectorstore_status`` is a ``[bool, bool]`` tuple.

Kept for backward compatibility with v1 API consumers. v2 routes convert
to :class:`InfraV2` before responding.

tenant_id.title: Tenant Id
tenant_id.description: Tenant identifier when status is queried for a specific tenant

InfraStatusResponse.title: InfraStatusResponse
InfraStatusResponse.description: Response for deployed infrastructure status checks (v1 shape).

tenant_id.title: Tenant Id
tenant_id.description: Tenant identifier when status is queried for a specific tenant

InfraStatusResponseV2.title: InfraStatusResponseV2
InfraStatusResponseV2.description:
V2 response for deployed infrastructure status checks.

Identical to :class:`InfraStatusResponse` except ``infra.vectorstore_status``
is a typed :class:`VectorstoreStatus` object rather than a 2-tuple.

ready_for_ingestion.title: Ready For Ingestion
ready_for_ingestion.description: True when the tenant is ready for ingestion and search. Derived from vectorstore_status.knowledge and vectorstore_status.memories.

InfraV2.title: InfraV2
InfraV2.description: V2 infra shape with ``vectorstore_status`` as a typed object.

type.description: Whether to list normal sources or user memories.

source_ids.title: Source Ids
source_ids.description: Optional list of IDs to fetch (max 100). If omitted, returns all.

page.title: Page
page.description: Page number to retrieve (1-indexed). Defaults to 1.

page_size.title: Page Size
page_size.description: Number of items per page (1-100). Defaults to 50.

filters.description: Optional filters. Provide key-value pairs to match against tenant_metadata, document_metadata, and/or source-level fields (title, type, description, url, timestamp).

include_fields.title: Include Fields
include_fields.description: Optional list of source fields to include in the response. When provided, only the specified fields (plus id, tenant_id, sub_tenant_id which are always returned) will be populated; all other fields will have their default/empty values. This reduces payload size and improves performance. Allowed values: attachments, content, description, document_metadata, note, relations, tenant_metadata, timestamp, title, type, url. Omit or pass null to return all fields. Only applies to type=knowledge; ignored for type=memory.

ListContentRequest.title: ListContentRequest
ListContentRequest.description:
Unified request model for listing either sources or user memories.

Supports **pagination** (page / page_size) and optional **filters**
on tenant_metadata and document_metadata.

If `source_ids` is provided and non-empty, only those IDs are returned
(pagination and filters still apply).

success.title: Success
success.description: Indicates whether the memory listing operation was successful

user_memories.title: User Memories
user_memories.description: Array of user memories for the current page

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of user memories matching the query

pagination.description: Pagination metadata for navigating through results.

ListUserMemoriesResponse.title: ListUserMemoriesResponse
ListUserMemoriesResponse.description: Response model for listing all user memories.

text.title: Text
text.description: Text content to ingest as memory.

MemoryIngestRequest.title: MemoryIngestRequest
MemoryIngestRequest.description: Request body for memory ingestion (text content).

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Optional unique identifier. Auto-generated if not provided.

title.title: Title
title.description: Display title for this memory item.

text.title: Text
text.description: Raw text or markdown content to be indexed.
examples:
# Introduction

This is the document content.

user_assistant_pairs.title: User Assistant Pairs
user_assistant_pairs.description: Array of user/assistant conversation pairs to store as memory.

is_markdown.title: Is Markdown
is_markdown.description: Whether the text is markdown formatted.

infer.title: Infer
infer.description: If true, process and extract additional insights/inferences from the contentbefore indexingUseful for extracting implicit information from conversations

custom_instructions.title: Custom Instructions
custom_instructions.description: Custom instructions to guide inference processing.

user_name.title: User Name
user_name.description: User's name for personalization in conversation pairs.

expiry_time.title: Expiry Time
expiry_time.description: Optional TTL in seconds for this memory.

metadata.description: Key-value metadata attached to this memory.

additional_metadata.description: Additional key-value metadata for this memory.

tenant_metadata.title: Tenant Metadata
tenant_metadata.description:
JSON string containing tenant-level document metadata (e.g., department, compliance_tag)

Example: > "{"department":"Finance","compliance_tag":"GDPR"}"

document_metadata.title: Document Metadata
document_metadata.description:
JSON string containing document-specific metadata (e.g., title, author, file_id). If file_id is not provided, the system will generate an ID automatically.

Example: > "{"title":"Q1 Report.pdf","author":"Alice Smith","file_id":"custom_file_123"}"

relations.description: Forcefully connect 2 sources based on cortex source ids or common properties.

MemoryItem.title: MemoryItem
MemoryItem.description:
Represents a single memory item for ingestion.
Supports raw text, markdown, and user/assistant conversation pairs.

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Unique identifier for the ingested source.

title.title: Title
title.description: Title of the memory if provided.

infer.title: Infer
infer.description: Whether inference was requested for this memory.

error_code.title: Error Code
error_code.description: Structured error code (e.g. 'E9001') if ingestion failed. Stable across pods/processes.

MemoryResultItem.title: MemoryResultItem
MemoryResultItem.description: Result for a single ingested memory item.

MilvusDataType.title: MilvusDataType
MilvusDataType.description: Milvus data types mapped from Weaviate schema.

page_size.title: Page Size
page_size.description: Number of items returned per page.

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of items matching the query.

PaginationMeta.title: PaginationMeta
PaginationMeta.description: Pagination metadata returned with every paginated response.

PipelineRunMetadata.title: PipelineRunMetadata
PipelineRunMetadata.description: Timeline of one or more ingestion runs for a (tenant, doc) pair.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

question.title: Question
question.description: The question to answer based on indexed sources

max_chunks.title: Max Chunks
max_chunks.description: Maximum number of context chunks to retrieve

mode.description: Retrieval mode: 'fast' for single query, 'thinking' for multi-query with reranking

search_mode.description: What to search: 'knowledge' for documents or 'memory' for user memories

include_graph_context.title: Include Graph Context
include_graph_context.description: Whether to include knowledge graph context for enhanced answers

search_forceful_relations.title: Search Forceful Relations
search_forceful_relations.description: Whether to fetch forceful-relation extra context in thinking mode

extra_context.title: Extra Context
extra_context.description: Additional context to guide retrieval and answer generation

model.title: Model
model.description: Specific model to use (defaults to provider's default model)

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: LLM temperature for answer generation (lower = more focused)

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens for the generated answer

QnASearchRequest.title: QnASearchRequest
QnASearchRequest.description: Request model for the QnA search API.

answer.title: Answer
answer.description: The AI-generated answer based on retrieved context

chunks.title: Chunks
chunks.description: Retrieved context chunks used to generate the answer

graph_context.description: Knowledge graph context (entity paths and chunk relations)

model_used.title: Model Used
model_used.description: The LLM model used for answer generation

QnASearchResponse.title: QnASearchResponse
QnASearchResponse.description: Response model for the QnA search API.

QueryBy.title: QueryBy
QueryBy.description: Query method to use for retrieval.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

query.title: Query
query.description: Search terms to find relevant content

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

recency_bias.title: Recency Bias
recency_bias.description: Preference for newer content (0.0 = no bias, 1.0 =             strong recency preference)

num_related_chunks.title: Num Related Chunks
num_related_chunks.description: Number of related content chunks to include

graph_context.title: Graph Context
graph_context.description: Enable graph context for query results (on by default on v2; set to false to omit).

query_forceful_relations.title: Query Forceful Relations
query_forceful_relations.description: Whether to search for forceful relations in thinking mode to augment context

additional_context.title: Additional Context
additional_context.description: Additional context provided by the user to guide retrieval

query_apps.title: Query Apps
query_apps.description: Temporary retrieval vertical switch. When True, a parallel app-aware retrieval lane runs alongside the standard lane and results are fused into one ranked list. When False (default), current behavior is preserved exactly. Memory searches ignore this flag.

metadata_filters.title: Metadata Filters
metadata_filters.description: Optional key-value pairs to filter search results by metadata fields. Top-level keys are matched against tenant_metadata and must correspond to fields defined in tenant_metadata_schema during tenant creation. To filter on document-level fields you sent as 'additional_metadata' at ingestion, nest filters under an 'additional_metadata' key: {'additional_metadata': {'source': 'account_plan'}}. 'document_metadata' is also accepted as a legacy alias for the nested key. Both filter types support exact match filtering and can be combined.

type.description: What to search: 'knowledge', 'memory', or 'all' (both merged). Mirrors the same values used by /context/ingest's `type` field.

operator.description: Text search operator (only applies when query_by='text'): 'or', 'and', or 'phrase'

QueryRequest.title: QueryRequest
QueryRequest.description:
Unified query request with query_by and source selection.

Overrides ``graph_context`` to default ``True`` .

embeddings.title: Embeddings
embeddings.description: Embedding payloads containing ids and vectors

RawEmbeddingDocument.title: RawEmbeddingDocument
RawEmbeddingDocument.description: A raw embedding document for direct insert/upsert operations.

embedding.description: Embedding payload with chunk id and vector (if set)

RawEmbeddingSearchResult.title: RawEmbeddingSearchResult
RawEmbeddingSearchResult.description: Search result for raw embedding collections.

RawEmbeddingVector.title: RawEmbeddingVector
RawEmbeddingVector.description: Embedding payload containing the chunk identifier and vector.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

query.title: Query
query.description: Search terms to find relevant content

max_results.title: Max Results
max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

recency_bias.title: Recency Bias
recency_bias.description: Preference for newer content (0.0 = no bias, 1.0 =             strong recency preference)

num_related_chunks.title: Num Related Chunks
num_related_chunks.description: Number of related content chunks to include

graph_context.title: Graph Context
graph_context.description: Enable graph context for search results

search_forceful_relations.title: Search Forceful Relations
search_forceful_relations.description: Whether to search for forceful relations in thinking mode to augment context

additional_context.title: Additional Context
additional_context.description: Additional context provided by the user to guide retrieval

search_apps.title: Search Apps
search_apps.description: Temporary retrieval vertical switch. When True, a parallel app-aware retrieval lane runs alongside the standard lane and results are fused into one ranked list. When False (default), current behavior is preserved exactly. Memory searches ignore this flag.

metadata_filters.title: Metadata Filters
metadata_filters.description: Optional key-value pairs to filter search results by metadata fields. Top-level keys are matched against tenant_metadata and must correspond to fields defined in tenant_metadata_schema during tenant creation. To filter on document-level fields you sent as 'additional_metadata' at ingestion, nest filters under an 'additional_metadata' key: {'additional_metadata': {'source': 'account_plan'}}. 'document_metadata' is also accepted as a legacy alias for the nested key. Both filter types support exact match filtering and can be combined.

RecallSearchRequest.title: RecallSearchRequest
RecallSearchRequest.description: Request body for full recall (sources) and recall preferences (memories).

context.title: Context
context.description: Rich contextual description of the relationship with surrounding information, details about how/why/when, and any relevant background. Should be comprehensive enough to understand the relationship without referring back to source.

temporal_details.title: Temporal Details
temporal_details.description: Temporal timing information extracted from text (e.g., 'last week', 'in 2023', 'yesterday')

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Timestamp when this relation was introduced

relationship_id.title: Relationship Id
relationship_id.description: Unique ID for this relationship from graph database

chunk_id.title: Chunk Id

source_entity_id.title: Source Entity Id
source_entity_id.description: The entity ID of source node

target_entity_id.title: Target Entity Id
target_entity_id.description: The entity ID of target node

RelationEvidence.description: Single piece of evidence for a relationship between two entities

sources.title: Sources
sources.description: Deduplicated source documents corresponding to the returned chunks

additional_context.title: Additional Context
additional_context.description: Map of chunk_uuid to VectorStoreChunk for extra context from forcefully related sources. Use chunk.extra_context_ids to look up chunks: extra_context[id] for id in chunk.extra_context_ids.

RetrievalResult.title: RetrievalResult
RetrievalResult.description: Result of a hybrid search retrieval operation.

group_id.title: Group Id
group_id.description: Path group identifier (e.g., 'p_0') for chunk mapping

source_chunk_ids.title: Source Chunk Ids
source_chunk_ids.description: Input chunk IDs whose graph traversal produced this path

ids.title: Ids
ids.description: List of source IDs to delete.

SourceDeleteRequest.description: Request to delete sources by their IDs.

SourceDeleteResultItem.title: SourceDeleteResultItem
SourceDeleteResultItem.description: Result for a single source deletion.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Source ID of the file to fetch

mode.description: Fetch mode: 'content' returns file content, 'url' returns presigned URL, 'both' returns both

expiry_seconds.title: Expiry Seconds
expiry_seconds.description: Expiry time in seconds for presigned URL (60-604800, default: 3600)

SourceFetchRequest.title: SourceFetchRequest

content.title: Content

presigned_url.title: Presigned Url
presigned_url.description: Presigned URL to access the file (if mode includes 'url')

size_bytes.title: Size Bytes
size_bytes.description: Size of the file in bytes

is_truncated.title: Is Truncated
is_truncated.description: Whether the result was truncated due to the limit

next_cursor.title: Next Cursor
next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for fetching the next page. Pass this value as the 'cursor' query parameter to continue pagination. None when there are no more results.

success.title: Success
success.description: Indicates whether the request was successful

message.title: Message
message.description: Response message describing the operation result

title.title: Title
title.description: Short human-readable title for the source

type.title: Type
type.description: Category of the source (e.g., document, email, ticket)

url.title: Url
url.description: Canonical URL or reference link associated with the source

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Creation or last-updated timestamp in ISO-8601 format

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Tenant-level metadata set at ingestion time

additional_metadata.title: Additional Metadata
additional_metadata.description: Document-level metadata set at ingestion time

app_kind.title: App Kind
app_kind.description: App-source kind discriminator (email, message, ticket, knowledge_base, comment, custom)

app_provider.title: App Provider
app_provider.description: App provider name (e.g. gmail, slack, jira, notion)

app_external_id.title: App External Id
app_external_id.description: Provider-scoped stable identifier for the source item

id.title: Id
id.description: Stable, unique identifier for the source. If omitted, one may be generated upstream.

sub_tenant_id.title: Sub Tenant Id
sub_tenant_id.description: Optional sub-tenant identifier used to organize data within a tenant. If omitted, the default sub-tenant created during tenant setup will be used.

title.title: Title
title.description: Short human-readable title for the source.

type.title: Type
type.description: High-level category of the source (e.g., document, email, ticket).

description.title: Description
description.description: Optional long-form description providing additional context.

note.title: Note
note.description: Free-form notes for internal use or ingestion hints.

url.title: Url
url.description: Canonical URL or reference link associated with the source.

timestamp.title: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Creation or last-updated timestamp of the source in ISO-8601 format.

content.description: Primary content payload used for indexing and retrieval.

tenant_metadata.title: Tenant Metadata
tenant_metadata.description:
JSON string containing tenant-level document metadata (e.g., department, compliance_tag)

Example: > "{"department":"Finance","compliance_tag":"GDPR"}"

document_metadata.title: Document Metadata
document_metadata.description:
JSON string containing document-specific metadata (e.g., title, author, file_id). If file_id is not provided, the system will generate an ID automatically.

Example: > "{"title":"Q1 Report.pdf","author":"Alice Smith","file_id":"custom_file_123"}"

meta.title: Meta
meta.description: System-provided attributes (e.g., app_name, local file size) not intended for search filtering.

attachments.title: Attachments

relations.description: Forcefully connect 2 sources based on cortex source ids or common properties.

error_code.title: Error Code

sub_tenant_ids.title: Sub Tenant Ids
sub_tenant_ids.description: Unique sub-tenant IDs that have indexed data in the tenant's collections

SubTenantIdsResponse.description: Response for listing unique sub-tenant IDs in a tenant (from Milvus).

SuperNodeResponse.description: Response model for the super-node detection endpoint.

tenant_id.title: Tenant Id

message.title: Message
message.default: Tenant creation started in the background. Use GET /tenants/infra/status?tenant_id=... to check progress.

TenantCreateAcceptedResponse.title: TenantCreateAcceptedResponse
TenantCreateAcceptedResponse.description: Response when tenant creation is accepted and runs in the background.

embeddings_dimension.title: Embeddings Dimension
embeddings_dimension.description: Embedding dimensions for embeddings tenant. Not required for non-embeddings (is_embeddings_tenant=False) tenants

tenant_metadata_schema.title: Tenant Metadata Schema
tenant_metadata_schema.description: Schema definition for tenant metadata fields. Each field can be configured for: filtering (enable_match), semantic search (enable_dense_embedding), and/or keyword search (enable_sparse_embedding). Fields with embeddings enabled must be VARCHAR type.

TenantCreateRequest.title: TenantCreateRequest
TenantCreateRequest.description:
Request model for creating a tenant with optional metadata schema.

The tenant_metadata_schema allows you to define custom fields that will be indexed
in Milvus with configurable search capabilities:

message.title: Message

title.title: Title

ids.title: Ids

V2SourceDeleteRequest.title: V2SourceDeleteRequest
V2SourceDeleteRequest.description:
v2 delete request — a flat body that carries the bucket selector inline.

Replaces the legacy nested ``{"request": {...}, "type": ...}`` body so every
field (``tenant_id``, ``sub_tenant_id``, ``ids``, ``type``) lives at the top
level of the request body.

content.title: Content
content.description: File content as string (if mode includes 'content')

content_base64.title: Content Base64
content_base64.description: File content as base64 (for binary files, if mode includes 'content')

presigned_url.title: Presigned Url

size_bytes.title: Size Bytes
size_bytes.description: Size of the file in bytes

V2SourceFetchResponse.title: V2SourceFetchResponse
V2SourceFetchResponse.description: Response for a fetch request. Mirrors ``SourceFetchResponse`` with ``id``.

results.title: Results
results.description: List of upload results for each source.

failed_count.title: Failed Count
failed_count.description: Number of sources that failed to upload.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique identifier for the uploaded source.

error_code.title: Error Code
error_code.description: Structured error code (E-code) if upload failed.

chunk_uuid.title: Chunk Uuid
chunk_uuid.description: Unique identifier for this content chunk

source_id.title: Source Id
source_id.description: Unique identifier for the source document

chunk_content.title: Chunk Content
chunk_content.description: The actual text content of this chunk
examples: This is a chunk of text from the source document.

source_type.title: Source Type
source_type.description: Type of the source document (file, webpage, etc.)

source_upload_time.title: Source Upload Time
source_upload_time.description: When the source document was originally uploaded

source_title.title: Source Title
source_title.description: Title or name of the source document

source_last_updated_time.title: Source Last Updated Time
source_last_updated_time.description: When the source document was last modified

layout.title: Layout
layout.description: Layout of the chunk in original document. You will generally receive        a stringified dict with 2 keys, `offsets` and `page`(optional). Offsets will have        `document_level_start_index` and `page_level_start_index`(optional)

relevancy_score.title: Relevancy Score
relevancy_score.description: Score indicating how relevant this chunk is to your search query,         with higher values indicating better matches

additional_metadata.title: Additional Metadata
additional_metadata.description: Document-level metadata set at ingestion time

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Tenant-level metadata set at ingestion time

extra_context_ids.title: Extra Context Ids
extra_context_ids.description: IDs of related chunks providing extra context (from forceful relations). Only present in thinking mode when sources have forceful relations.

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.description: Whether the knowledge vectorstore is provisioned.

memories.title: Memories
memories.description: Whether the memories vectorstore is provisioned.

url.title: Url
url.description: HTTPS URL Cortex will POST to. Must be reachable from Cortex servers.

event_types.title: Event Types
event_types.description: List of event types to subscribe to. Supported: ['indexing.status_changed']

signing_secret.title: Signing Secret
signing_secret.description: Optional secret for payload signing. When set, every delivery includes X-HydraDB-Signature: sha256=<hmac-sha256> computed as HMAC-SHA256(key=signing_secret, msg=raw_body). Minimum 16 characters. Omit to disable signing.
